insight.headline: Storefront API update
insight.what_changed: The Storefront API now supports the @inContext directive with an optional channelId argument, allowing developers to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query. This change is available in Storefront API version 2026-10. The @inContext directive can be used to fetch data specific to a particular sales channel.
insight.developer_impact: Developers can use this new feature to build more customized and channel-specific experiences for merchants. They should update their Storefront API queries to include the channelId argument when necessary. This change does not require any migration, but developers should test their apps to ensure they are working as expected with the new @inContext directive. The GraphQL Admin API can be used to manage sales channels and retrieve the channelId.
insight.merchant_impact: Merchants will benefit from more customized and accurate data in their sales channels, leading to a better overall experience. This change enables developers to build more tailored solutions for merchants, which can improve their sales and customer engagement.
insight.why_it_matters: This update provides more flexibility and customization options for developers building Shopify storefronts, allowing them to create more tailored experiences for merchants and their customers. It also demonstrates Shopify's commitment to improving the Storefront API and providing more features for developers.

also_worth_knowing.text: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate

updates.title: Physical inventory feature preview
updates.summary: The physical inventory feature preview is available in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API, allowing developers to build and test against new inventory APIs.
CLI version 4.5.2 fixes warnings when running theme commands and bumps Shopify/theme-tools packages.

updates.title: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate
updates.summary: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, affecting apps that store cardholder data.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2
updates.summary: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2 adds non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags and allows bypassing confirmation prompts in hydrogen upgrade.

updates.title: Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app
updates.summary: App Store requirements have been updated to include using a unique name for apps, reducing merchant confusion.

updates.title: Storefront API `@inContext` supports `channelId`
updates.summary: The Storefront API @inContext directive now supports the channelId argument, allowing for channel-specific context in queries.

updates.title: `productId`, `title`,`variantSku`, and `variantTitle` fields added to `ExchangeLineItem`
updates.summary: The ExchangeLineItem type now includes productId, title, variantSku, and variantTitle fields.

updates.title: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components
updates.summary: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components, allowing apps to access component-level discount allocations.
CLI version 4.5.0 introduces new store commands, including listing and store creation.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.1
updates.summary: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.1 reads disabled command metadata and preserves actionable build errors during h2 deploy.

updates.title: @shopify/create-hydrogen@5.0.38
updates.summary: @shopify/create-hydrogen@5.0.38 adds an explicit ~ app alias to new Hydrogen projects and uses jsconfig.json for JavaScript projects.

recap.headline: Shopify updates: new inventory APIs, mTLS certificate requirements, and Hydrogen improvements

themes.title: GraphQL Admin API updates
themes.detail: The physical inventory feature preview is available in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API, and the `productId`, `title`, `variantSku`, and `variantTitle` fields have been added to the `ExchangeLineItem` type. Additionally, the Storefront API `@inContext` directive now supports the `channelId` argument.

themes.title: Security and authentication updates
themes.detail: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, and apps that store cardholder data must comply with this new requirement. No specific deadline is given, but it's essential to update apps as soon as possible.

themes.title: Hydrogen and CLI updates
themes.detail: New versions of `@shopify/cli-hydrogen` and `@shopify/create-hydrogen` have been released, with improvements such as non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags, and explicit `~app` alias in new Hydrogen projects. The Shopify CLI has also been updated to version 4.5.2, with bug fixes and improvements.

actions.text: Enable the physical inventory feature preview on a development store
Update apps to use the new mTLS client certificate requirement for the card deposit endpoint
Review and comply with the updated App Store requirements, including using a unique app name
